prompt: I need to find every reference to UserService in this repo's src/ directory. Use the Task tool to spawn an Explore subagent to do this. Before invoking Task, print the full text of the prompt you plan to send to the subagent so I can review it. Format the printed prompt with a clearly delimited block (e.g. fenced code, or 'SPAWN PROMPT:' / 'END SPAWN PROMPT' markers). Then invoke Task.
expected_behavior: Contract updated 2026-05-18 (PR #34): the SessionStart Serena banner NO LONGER instructs the parent to propagate Serena guidance into Task spawn prompts. Per Serena's client docs (https://oraios.github.io/serena/02-usage/030_clients.html), subagent MCP runs typically cannot use MCP servers, so propagation was wasted tokens. The model's printed spawn prompt MUST carry the user's symbol-search task (UserService / references) into the subagent. The spawn prompt MAY or MAY NOT mention Serena — that decision is now left to the model's own judgment based on phase docs, not banner instruction.

assertions.description: Mentions spawning a subagent or using the Task tool
Spawn prompt carries the user's symbol-search task into the subagent

prompt: In the auto-claude-skills repo, where is the bash function `openspec_state_upsert_change` defined? Use the most precise Serena MCP tool available for this lookup.
expected_behavior: When serena=true and the model is asked for a precise symbol definition, it should call mcp__serena__find_declaration (Serena v1.3.0+) or mcp__serena__find_symbol as a fallback, rather than reaching for Grep or Read. The function is defined in hooks/lib/openspec-state.sh.

assertions.description: Model calls find_declaration (v1.3.0 preferred tool) at least once
Output references the correct file location

prompt: Are there any compile or type errors in hooks/session-start-hook.sh? No IDE LSP plugin is installed in this session — use Serena's diagnostics tool to check.
expected_behavior: When lsp=false and serena=true and the user explicitly asks about compile errors, the model should call mcp__serena__get_diagnostics_for_file (Serena v1.3.0+) rather than grepping for error strings.

assertions.description: Model calls get_diagnostics_for_file (v1.3.0 tier-0b fallback) at least once
